name: Hot Chocolate Dry Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Put 1/3 cup of mix into cup and add boiling or very hot water.
Stir and enjoy.

name: Kicky Hot Chocolate
directions: Mix sugar, cocoa and salt in saucepan.
Stir in water.
Cook and stir over medium heat until mixture boils.
Boil and stir 2 minutes.
Stir in milk and heat, do not boil.
Remove from heat, add vanilla.
Pour into mugs.
Add a shot of schnapps.
Top off with whipped cream and a sprinkle of cinnamon.

name: Minted Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Place candy in a heavy-duty zip-top plastic bag; crush candy with a mallet.
Combine crushed candy and remaining ingredients.
Store in an airtight container.
To serve, combine 1/4 cup cocoa mix and 3/4 cup boiling water; stir well.

name: Mexican Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine sugar, cocoa and cinnamon in a small, heavy-duty saucepan; gradually stir in milk.
Warm over medium heat, stirring constantly, until hot (do not boil).
Remove from heat, stir in vanilla.
Beat with wire whisk until frothy.
Serve.

similar.name: Aztec Hot Chocolate (For the Crock Pot)

name: New England Hot Chocolate
directions: In a large saucepan, combine sugar, cocoa, and salt.
Stir in hot water and butter; bring to a boil.
Add the milk, maple flavoring, vanilla and 8 marshmallows.
Heat through, stirring occasionally, until marshmallows melt.
Pour into mugs and top each with a marshmallow.

name: Rich Hot Chocolate
directions: Stir chocolate and water in a medium saucepan over low heat until chocolate has melted (mixture will thicken).
Gradually whisk in milk, then sugar and salt until evenly blended.
Heat through and serve.

name: Chocolatey Hot Cocoa for One
directions: In a small, heavy saucepan, stir together the cocoa and sugar, crushing any lumps.
First by approximately Tablespoons, and then in a slow steady stream, stir in the milk.
Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly and scraping the bottom of the pan, just until bubbles appear at the sides.
Remove from the heat and stir in the vanilla or liqueur.
Top with ground nutmeg or cinnamon, whipped cream or marshmallows.

similar.name: Rich Hot Cocoa for Two With Flavor Variations

name: Old Fashioned Cocoa
directions: In a small pan, combine cocoa and sugar, whisk in half and half till smooth.
Over low heat, whisk in milk and vanilla, bring to a simmer.
Pour into hot mugs and top with cream and chocolate.
To add variety add cinnamon and nutmeg before adding milk, or use peppermint extract in place of vanilla.

name: Christmas Coffee
directions: Over medium heat, boil sugar, cocoa, cinnamon and water for 1 minute, stirring frequently. Add to coffee, stir and serve immediately. Stir again if served later.
Serve with whipped topping, milk and sugar.
Good with a splash of Brandy, Frangelico, Kahlua or Bailey's, if desired.

similar.name: Rich Hot Cocoa for Two With Flavor Variations

name: Peppermint Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine all ingredients (except the shaved chocolate and whipped cream) and carefully bring to a simmer.
Serve hot, garnished with a dollop of whipped cream and chocolate shavings.

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: mix ingredients.
add about 1/4 cup mix per coffee cup. If you want it more chocolatey add more, if it is to strong add less.
Top with hot hot water, and mix.
don't forget the marshmallows.
Enjoy.

name: Rich Hot Cocoa
directions: Measure water into a 2-quart sauce pan.  Add sugar, cocoa and salt.  Heat water over medium heat. Stir mixture until cocoa dissolves completely. Gently bring mixture to a boil.  Reduce the heat to a fast simmer and simmer it for about 5 minutes.    Finally add the evaporated milk and stir to blend.  Reheat briefly, but don't boil.  Pour the hot chocolate into small mugs.
This recipe makes 4  6-ounce size servings.
I added a spoonful of whipped topping on the top of each filled mug.  It was delicious!

name: Mexican Hot   Chocolate
directions: In a medium sized saucepan, heat the milk over medium heat until scalding, but not boiling.
Break the chocolate into pieces. Add to the hot milk along with the cinnamon, sugar and salt. Stir until melted. Beat with a wire whisk until frothy.
Add the vanilla. Continue to whisk.
Pour into 4 mugs. Garnish with a cinnamon stick.

name: Candy Cane Hot Chocolate
directions: In a large bowl measure and sift powdered sugar, unsweetned cocoa, coffee creamer,salt and skim milk powder.
Break up and pulse in food processor til candy canes are very fine. Cover your ears its gonna get noisy!
Mix all together and store in air tight container until ready to serve.
To make hot chocolate mix 1/3 cup hot chocolate mix in a cup of boiling water. Top with whipped cream!

For a gift basket idea I make a batch of just plain hot chocolate, one batch with candy cane added and one batch with mini marshmallows. For a batch -one cup of mini marhmallows. Fill three decorative air tight containers with one of each of these kinds label with instructions for making, add a cute mug, nice gift for the holidays.
About 14 candy canes or 175 gram box will yield one cup.

name: Sinfully Rich & Delicious Hot Chocolate
directions: Place the milk, chocolate and sugar in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk periodically until the mixture reaches the boiling point and is foamy.
Remove from heat and stir in vanilla.
For more foam, use a wire whisk or hand held blender to whip the hot chocolate.
If used, add any optional toppings.

name: Aztec Chili Hot Chocolate
directions: Simmer milk in a saucepan with vanilla bean, cinnamon and chili. Heat through for about a minute.
Whisk in grated chocolate, and continue to simmer until melted.
Remove from heat and let 'steep' for another 2 minutes.
Strain out the spices and serve.

name: Hot Cocoa With Sweet Potato
directions: Combine the milk, sweet potato puree, chocolate syrup, salt and spice in a blender and blend till smooth.
Transfer to a saucepan and bring to a simmer.
Pour into mugs and top with marshmallows.
Enjoy.  =).

name: Amaretto Hot Chocolate for Two
directions: Put milk in a 4 Cup measuring cup, or other larger microwave safe dish (to leave room for stiring).
Add cocoa, sugar and almond extract.
Microwave for 3 minutes or until hot.
Whisk (or just stir) until combined.
Serve topped with whipped cream or marshmallows.
Enjoy!

name: Mexican Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine hot milk, chocolate, sugar, coffee and salt in a blender.  Cover, blend at high speed, until chocolate is melted and sugar dissolves.
In a large saucepan,  combine blended ingredients with remaining ingredients.  Heat, stirring constantly.  Blend small amounts of hot mixture just before serving.

similar.name: Aztec Hot Chocolate (For the Crock Pot)

name: Old-Fashioned Hot Chocolate
directions: IF USING CHOCOLATE:
Grate chocolate (manually or in food processor fitted with steel blades). Add in corn starch, sugar, and salt.
IF USING COCOA:
Mix solid ingredients, butter, and a little warmed milk to make a smooth paste; add remaining milk.
In a one-quart, heavy-bottomed, non-aluminum saucepan, heat milk over medium low heat, stirring often with small whisk, until it is steaming hot. Carefully add chocolate mixture.

Continue cooking mixture, stirring almost constantly with whisk and scraping bottom and sides of pot with heat resistant spatula frequently. Mixture will steam for several (15-20) minutes before coming to a boil, and will thicken slightly.
When mixture boils, continue cooking and stirring for only 30 to 45 seconds.
Remove from heat; whisk in vanilla, sugar (as much as you want) and any optional ingredients.
TO SERVE:
Divide among small 4 cups or mugs, and mix 1 tablespoon of skim milk in each mug to achieve desired consistency; you may need to warm in microwave once skim is added.

similar.name: Aztec Hot Chocolate (For the Crock Pot)

name: Family Batch Hot Chocolate
directions: Put 2 cups of milk into a saucepan or crockpot with the cocoa, vanilla, salt, and sugar/Splenda.
Heat slowly (low heat on your stove, high heat in a crockpot), stirring vigorously with a wire whisk until sugar dissolves and cocoa is fully wetted.
Add remaining milk and continue to heat gently (very low heat on stove, low heat in crockpot), stirring frequently and vigorously.
When steaming hot (do not boil), serve, adding any of your favorite hot chocolate accessories -- mini-marshmallows, whipped cream, cinnamon sticks, etc.
Can be held for a while in a crockpot on warm but may develop a skin. If made in a saucepan best to do any necessary reheating in the microwave.
Note: For an even richer drink substitute a cup of heavy cream for 1 cup of milk.
Note: Adults may enjoy a shot of peppermint schnapps, Kahlua, spiced rum, or other favorite liquor as an addition.
Note: Substituting Dutch Processed cocoa for regular cocoa gives a distinctly different flavor you might want to try.

similar.name: Rich Hot Cocoa for Two With Flavor Variations

name: Maple Hot Chocolate
directions: In a large saucepan, combine sugar, cocoa and salt. Stir in hot water and butter; bring to a boil. Add the milk, maple flavoring, vanilla and 8 marshmallows. Heat through, stirring occasionally, until marshmallows are melted. Ladle into mugs and top each with a marshmallow. Yield: 4 servings.

name: Rich Mexican Hot Cocoa
directions: Heat the 2 cups of milk, cocoa powder, sugar, cinnamon, and butter on medium.  Use a whisk to make sure the cocoa powder doesn't clump.
Put the corn starch in a small cup and add 1/3 cup of milk.  Mix together with a fork untill it is completely dissolved.
Add corn starch mixture to cocoa mixture and whisk.  Heat untill thickened but don't boil.
Take off the heat and add vanilla.
Put 10 marshmallow into each of 2 mugs and pour hot cocoa over.

name: White Cocoa
directions: 1) Put chopped white chocolate in a bowl. Set aside. Combine milk, cream, and pepper in medium saucepan. Place over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Heat until it starts to simmer; do not let it boil.
2) Pour heated mixture over white chocolate, let sit 1 minute, then mix with a whisk until all of the chocolate is melted.
3) Ladle cocoa into mugs. Garnish each one with two marshmallows.

name: Orange-Almond Rich 'n' Thick Hot Chocolate
directions: Whisk together cornstarch and 1/2 cup milk until smooth.
Cook remaining 3 1/2 cups milk in a large, nonaluminum saucepan over medium heat until bubbles appear around edge of saucepan (about 4 minutes; do not boil).
Whisk in chocolate, honey, orange juice, almond liqueur, vanilla extract, and salt until blended and smooth. Whisk in cornstarch mixture.
Bring milk mixture to a light boil, whisking frequently (about 4 minutes). Remove from heat. Let cool slightly. (Mixture will thicken as it cools.) Serve immediately.

name: Powdered Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together in an airtight container and shake until all ingrediants are thoroughly mixed.  (Ex: And Empty Ice Cream Pail).
To Mix:  1/3 Cup of Mix in Mug, Fill with Hot Water and Stir,.
To With Miniature Marshmellows if desired.
Enjoy!

name: Hot Chocolate Malted
directions: Mix all ingredients, except the last two optional ones, in a slow cooker.
Heat on high for 3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Taste before serving.  Depending on cocoa mix you might need to add more liquid.  (Be sure to heat any additional liquid in the microwave before adding.).
Ladle into cups & top with whipped cream/marshmallows if you wish.

name: Rich Hot Cocoa for Two With Flavor Variations
directions: Combine sugar, cocoa, and water in small saucepan.  Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Add milk and heat to serving temperature.  Do not boil.
Add vanilla; serve in mugs and top with spoonful of wihhpped cream or marshmallows.
Stir or blend in flavor variations if desired.

name: Anyday Holiday Coffee
directions: Brew the coffee.
Drop the cinnamon stick into the cup.
Add coffee, peppermint extract and Splenda.
Add milk if using.
Stir and enjoy.

name: Spiced Hot Chocolate
directions: Place milk, cocoa, and sugar in saucepan. Heat to boiling, then turn off heat. Stir in nutmeg, chili powder and stir to mix. Pour into 4 mugs and place cinnamon sticks in mugs, if using. Garnish with whipped cream and sprinkle with cinnamon if desired. Enjoy!

similar.name: Aztec Hot Chocolate (For the Crock Pot)

name: Double Chocolate Hot Chocolate
directions: In a medium bowl, combine the bittersweet chocolate with the milk chocolate. Add the boiling water and let stand for 1 minute. Whisk the chocolate mixture until smooth.
In a small saucepan, bring the milk and heavy cream just to a simmer. Add the chocolate mixture and whisk over medium heat until hot.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and add whiskey. Pour the hot chocolate into demitasse cups or small mugs, garnish with marshmallows and serve.

name: Almond Hot Cocoa / Hot Chocolate
directions: In a large sauce pan, heat milk over medium-low.
While heating Milk, Chop up Hershey bars. (And peppermint, if applicable.).
Once milk is hot (Do NOT Boil), stir in All other ingredients. (Make sure to use only one flavoring.).
Don't stop gently stirring.
Take a spoon and taste it.
Add sugar slowly, tasting as you go.
Once you get to where you like it, stop.
Turn off burners, and whisk until frothy. (Note: I usually put it in a blender to make the whisking that much easier. Shh, don't tell my Grandma.).
Pour into mugs and enjoy.

similar.name: Rich Hot Cocoa for Two With Flavor Variations

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: In a bowl, combine the first six ingredients. Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to 3 months.
To prepare: Dissolve 1/3 cup cocoa mix in boiling water. Top with miniature marshmallows, if desired.

name: Chocolate Milk Mix
directions: Mix: 1/3 cup of mix with 2/3 cup of hot water.

name: Aztec Hot Chocolate (For the Crock Pot)
directions: In a large bowl combine milk, half-and-half, coffee powder, the 1 teaspoon cinnamon, and the ground chipotle chile pepper. If desired, cover bowl and chill for up to 24 hours.

Transfer mixture to a 3-1/2- or 4-quart slow cooker. Stir in chocolate pieces. Cover and cook on low-heat setting for 4 hours or on high-heat setting for 2 hours, whisking vigorously once halfway through cooking time. Serve immediately or keep warm on warm setting or low-heat setting for up to 2 hours.
Before serving, whisk well. To serve, ladle beverage into heatproof mugs or cups. If desired, garnish each serving with whipped cream and/or sprinkle with additional cinnamon.
